items.text: norn-tui crate exists as a workspace member at crates/norn-tui/
Cargo.toml declares termina, pulldown-cmark, syntect, unicode-width, tokio dependencies
src/lib.rs declares public modules: app, input, render, tools, agents, events, terminal
norn-cli depends on norn-tui as a path dependency
norn-cli mode detection dispatches to norn-tui when stdin+stdout are TTYs and --print is not set
TerminalCaps struct detects and stores: true_colour, kitty_keyboard, synchronized_rendering, osc_hyperlinks, italic_support
Terminal enters raw mode on startup and restores on exit (including panic cleanup)
DECSTBM scroll region is set on startup, splitting the terminal into scroll region and fixed panel
DECSTBM is reissued whenever the fixed panel height changes
TUI refuses to start if DECSTBM or 256-colour are not available, printing an explanatory message and falling back to --print mode
TextDelta events write styled markdown output into the scroll region
ThinkingDelta events render with ANSI dim attribute in the scroll region
ToolCallDelta events accumulate per tool-call ID and render through the per-tool renderer on completion
Done events clear the streaming indicator and show usage summary in the fixed panel
Error events render with a distinct error style in the scroll region
Streaming indicator in the fixed panel shows generating status with elapsed time while the model is producing output
Bold text (**bold**) renders with ANSI bold attribute
Italic text (*italic*) renders with ANSI italic attribute, falling back to underline
Headers (# H1, ## H2) render with bold and size-appropriate formatting
Inline code (`code`) renders with distinct foreground colour
Fenced code blocks render with syntect syntax highlighting using the language hint
Unlabeled code blocks fall back to syntect find_syntax_by_first_line
Lists (bullet and numbered) render with appropriate indentation
Horizontal rules render as a line spanning the terminal width
Links render as OSC 8 hyperlinks when supported, bracketed text otherwise
Incomplete inline spans (bold, italic) buffer until the closing marker arrives before rendering
Code fences buffer from opening to closing marker, then render the complete block with highlighting
ToolRenderer trait defines the interface for all tool renderers: header_line() and body()
Bash renderer streams output with ANSI passthrough, shows exit code and duration on completion
Edit renderer shows unified diff with red/green colouring and blast-radius symbols on success
Edit renderer shows AST BLOCKED header with diagnostic errors (not diff) when edit is rolled back
Edit renderer shows COMMITTED with override attribution when AllowBrokenAst is active
ApplyPatch renderer shows diff hunks with +/- colouring
Search renderer shows file:line:content with highlighted matches grouped by file
Read renderer shows file path and line range, body hidden by default
Write renderer shows file path, line count, and AST status on one line
WebSearch renderer shows query and result count on one line
WebFetch renderer shows URL and content length on one line
SpawnAgent renderer creates a status line in the fixed panel, not the scroll region
WaitAgent is not rendered as a tool call — the wait is invisible to the user
Ctrl+O toggles global verbosity for future tool calls without affecting scrollback content
Enter submits input to the agent loop
Shift+Enter (Kitty keyboard) or Alt+Enter (fallback) inserts a newline and expands the input area
Input area grows upward, reissuing DECSTBM to shrink the scroll region
Escape clears the current input
Ctrl+C on empty input exits the TUI
Up/Down arrows cycle through input history when no autocomplete popup is open
Input history persists to ~/.norn/history.txt
/ at column 0 triggers slash command and skill completion
Slash command popup shows name, source tag (builtin/profile), and description
@ triggers file/directory path completion via nucleo fuzzy matching (nucleo-matcher dependency comes through the norn crate)
Autocomplete popup renders inside the fixed panel, growing upward with DECSTBM reissue
Autocomplete popup shows at most 8 rows with a count indicator for additional candidates
Tab accepts the current popup selection
Escape dismisses the autocomplete popup
No agent status lines appear in the single-agent case
Agent status lines show indent, icon, name, activity, token count, and elapsed time
Agent status icons distinguish running, idle, done, failed, and spawning states
Agent tree collapses to at most 5 visible lines with overflow summary
Root agent is always visible when children exist
Completed/failed agents hold their status line for 3 seconds before reclaim
Tab cycles focus between agents in the fixed panel
Enter on a focused agent switches the active tab to that agent
Tab switch replays the last N events from the target agent's EventStore into the scroll region
A separator line marks each tab switch in the scroll region
Assistant messages render as markdown through the D6 rendering pipeline
Thinking content renders with ANSI dim attribute, collapsed by default
Ctrl+E toggles thinking visibility and secondary schema fields for future output
Tool calls render through per-tool renderers (D7) with appropriate visual treatment per tool type
User messages render with a distinct prefix separating them from assistant output
Structured assistant messages with multiple schema fields render the primary field by default with secondary fields toggleable
Secondary schema fields render with labeled separators between sections
New event types with EventSchemaSet schemas render automatically via the labeled-section pattern
Fixed panel height is the sum of its active components (agent lines + indicator + popup + input + status bar)
Fixed panel redraws use synchronized rendering when available, cursor hide/show otherwise
Status bar shows model name, session info, token usage, and key hints
Fixed panel redraws do not affect scroll region content
True colour detected via COLORTERM or termina DA1, falls back to 256-colour palette mapping
Kitty keyboard protocol used for Shift+Enter when available, Alt+Enter otherwise
Synchronized rendering wraps fixed-panel redraws when available
OSC 8 hyperlinks used for file paths when available, bracketed text otherwise
Italic attribute used for markdown emphasis when available, underline otherwise
